goal: Long-horizon 10-step pick-place-stack plan with per-step sub-goal verification and retry-on-failure semantics. A single Franka Panda assembles a 3-block tower from three source bins, performs intermediate stack-height sub-goal checks, then transports the assembled stack to an outfeed bin. After each pick or place step the orchestrator calls get_world_transform + check_collisions to verify the sub-goal; if the sub-goal threshold is missed, execute_with_retry is invoked (max_retries=2 per step) to replay the same code patch with the LLM autonomous fix loop. The 10 sequenced steps are: (1) pick block A from bin1, (2) place block A on station, (3) pick block B from bin2, (4) place block B on top of A, (5) sub-goal stack-height check after step 4, (6) pick block C from bin3, (7) place block C on top of B, (8) sub-goal 3-block-stack stability check, (9) pick the assembled stack from station, (10) place stack in outfeed bin. Distinct from CP-NEW-assembly-line-4robot-handoff (multi-robot serial pipeline, no retry) and CP-NEW-yrkesroll-packer-box-seal (single-phase pack + lid, no retry) — this template is the FIRST canonical to exercise long-horizon orchestration with structured retry-on-failure.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-planning-10step-retry — first long-horizon canonical with per-step retry-on-failure:

1) Topology — single Franka Panda at [0, 0, 0.75] on a 1.2×0.8×0.75m work table. Three source bins arranged in an arc in front of the robot: Bin1 (block A) at [0.30, 0.40, 0.775], Bin2 (block B) at [0.45, 0.20, 0.775], Bin3 (block C) at [0.45, -0.20, 0.775]. Station (stack platform) at [-0.10, 0.30, 0.775] — flat Cube target for the 3-block tower. Outfeed bin at [-0.30, -0.30, 0.775]. All workspace inside Franka 0.85m reach (max horizontal distance 0.54m).

2) Three blocks — 0.05m cubes, mass 0.1 kg, rubber material for stable friction-stacking. BlockA in Bin1 at z=0.835, BlockB in Bin2 at z=0.835, BlockC in Bin3 at z=0.835. Station top z = 0.775 + 0.005 (platform thickness) = 0.780. Stack heights after each placement: BlockA on station → centre z=0.805 (top z=0.830). BlockB on A → centre z=0.855 (top z=0.880). BlockC on B → centre z=0.905 (top z=0.930). Sub-goal #1 (after step 4): top of stack must be within [0.870, 0.890] (i.e. 2-block tower height). Sub-goal #2 (after step 8): top of stack within [0.920, 0.940] (3-block tower height) AND check_collisions on /World/Station reports stable contact between the three blocks.

3) Step-by-step plan (10 steps, with implicit sub-goal verification after each):
   Step 1 — pick BlockA from Bin1 (setup_pick_place_controller with source_paths=[/World/BlockA], destination_path=/World/Station). Sub-goal: BlockA transform z within [0.795, 0.815] AND xy within [-0.13, -0.07] × [0.27, 0.33].
   Step 2 — place BlockA on station (handled inside the same setup_pick_place_controller call — step 1 and 2 are the two sub-phases of a single pick_place controller invocation). Sub-goal: same as step 1.
   Step 3 — pick BlockB from Bin2 (second setup_pick_place_controller call).
   Step 4 — place BlockB on top of BlockA. Sub-goal: BlockB transform z within [0.845, 0.865]; 2-block stack-height check via get_world_transform on /World/BlockB compared to /World/BlockA.
   Step 5 — explicit sub-goal verification: get_world_transform(/World/BlockB), assert z within tolerance; if FAIL, invoke execute_with_retry to replay the place-B-on-A patch.
   Step 6 — pick BlockC from Bin3 (third setup_pick_place_controller call).
   Step 7 — place BlockC on top of BlockB. Sub-goal: BlockC transform z within [0.895, 0.915].
   Step 8 — 3-block stability check: get_world_transform(/World/BlockC) + check_collisions(/World/BlockC) AND check_collisions(/World/BlockB). Sub-goal: BlockC z within tolerance AND check_collisions reports steady contact (no fall-through). If FAIL, execute_with_retry the stack-C-on-B patch.
   Step 9 — pick the assembled stack from station (treat /World/BlockA as the grasp target — Franka grasps the bottom block to lift the whole tower; the stack moves as a unit because rubber friction + low velocity keeps blocks adhered).
   Step 10 — place stack in /World/OutfeedBin. Sub-goal: all three blocks within outfeed bin footprint (xy tolerance 0.12m, z within bin).

4) Retry-on-failure mechanism — execute_with_retry is the primary retry tool. Pattern: for each step that performs a physical action, the orchestrator first executes the action via setup_pick_place_controller, then verifies the sub-goal via get_world_transform/check_collisions. If verification fails, the orchestrator calls execute_with_retry(code=<replay patch>, description=<step name>, max_retries=2). The autonomous fix loop reads any error from the previous attempt, asks the LLM for a fix, and replays. Retry counter is RESET between steps — step 4's retries do not eat step 7's budget. After 2 retries exhaust, the canonical reports per-step success/retry-count and the final outcome (success / partial-success-at-step-N / total-failure).

5) Partial-progress recovery — the template intentionally does NOT restart from step 1 on a step-N failure. Each retry replays only the failed step's code patch (e.g. step 4 retry replays the place-B-on-A pick_place call), not the upstream picks. This is the 'resume from failed step' semantics. The trade-off (documented in failure_modes #4): if the failure was caused by upstream state corruption (e.g. BlockA shifted during step 3 reach-over), retrying step 4 alone will hit the same failure. The mitigation is that an LLM-driven fix in execute_with_retry can detect this and emit a fix that includes a corrective re-place of BlockA before retrying step 4.

6) Sub-goal threshold tuning — z-tolerance ±0.010m per block stack height. Tighter tolerances (e.g. ±0.005m) would cause frequent false-positive retries on legitimate placements that wobble during settle; looser tolerances (±0.020m) would let unstable stacks pass verification and fail downstream at step 9. The ±0.010m threshold is calibrated to Franka cuRobo drop precision (~5-8mm) plus 1-2mm settle wobble. Documented as failure_mode #2.

7) Sim-control settle pauses — between each step's pick_place and the next step's sub-goal check, insert sim_control(action='step', step_count=60) (~1s sim-time at 60Hz) so the block has time to settle before its z-position is read. Without this, get_world_transform returns the position at gripper-release moment, before gravity has finished pulling the block onto the stack — false-negative sub-goal failure. Documented as failure_mode #3.

8) Physics — CPU dynamics (enable_gpu_dynamics=False) for determinism. Rubber material on all three blocks + station platform maximises friction-stacking. sleepThreshold=0.0 keeps blocks simulated through the full 10-step plan. Station platform is static (PhysicsCollisionAPI only, no RigidBodyAPI). All three blocks have full physics (RigidBody + Collision + Mass + PhysxRigidBody).

9) Verification — verify_args.stages encodes the four pick-place phases (step1-2: A→station; step3-4: B→A; step6-7: C→B; step9-10: stack→outfeed). simulate_args.target_path = /World/OutfeedBin; simulate_args.cube_paths = all three blocks. settle_state records the final 3-block stack in outfeed plus the per-step retry counters (all 0 on nominal-success run).

10) Structural tags — isaac:planning.long_horizon (new — first canonical for long-horizon plans), isaac:planning.retry_on_failure (new — exercises retry semantics), isaac:orchestration.10_step_sequence (new — explicit step-count tag for retrieval), isaac:robot.franka_panda, isaac:robot.fixed_base.arm, isaac:topology.single_station, isaac:pattern.stack_then_transport. pattern_hint='other' because long-horizon planning is not a primitive pick_place pattern — the discriminating signal is structural_tags.planning.*.

11) Comparison to siblings:
   - vs CP-NEW-assembly-line-4robot-handoff: that template has 4 robots in serial pipeline (3 hand-offs); this template has 1 robot doing 10 sequential steps (no hand-offs).
   - vs CP-NEW-yrkesroll-packer-box-seal: that template has 2 pick_place phases (pack + seal); this template has 4 pick_place phases (A, B, C placements + final transport) plus retry verification gates.
   - vs CP-NEW-yrkesroll-machinist-cnc-load: that template uses sim_control as a machining-cycle dwell; this template uses sim_control as a between-step settle pause for sub-goal verification.

12) Function-gate — simulate_args measures the end-state: BlockA, BlockB, BlockC all inside /World/OutfeedBin after duration_s=400 (long horizon: ~4 pick-place phases × ~75s each + retry slack).
code:
# -- Scene skeleton --------------------------------------------------------
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Robot work table (1.2 x 0.8 x 0.75m)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.6, 0.4, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# -- Physics scene: CPU dynamics for determinism ---------------------------
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# -- Franka Panda robot ----------------------------------------------------
# Reach ~0.85m. Bins/station/outfeed within 0.55m horizontal.
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# -- Three source bins (one per block) -------------------------------------
create_bin(
    prim_path="/World/Bin1",
    position=[0.30, 0.40, 0.775],
    size=[0.12, 0.12, 0.04],
)
create_bin(
    prim_path="/World/Bin2",
    position=[0.45, 0.20, 0.775],
    size=[0.12, 0.12, 0.04],
)
create_bin(
    prim_path="/World/Bin3",
    position=[0.45, -0.20, 0.775],
    size=[0.12, 0.12, 0.04],
)

# -- Stack station (flat platform where the 3-block tower assembles) -------
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Station",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[-0.10, 0.30, 0.775],
    scale=[0.08, 0.08, 0.005],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Station", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Station", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.35, 0.40, 0.45]])

# -- Outfeed bin (final destination for the assembled stack) ---------------
create_bin(
    prim_path="/World/OutfeedBin",
    position=[-0.30, -0.30, 0.775],
    size=[0.20, 0.20, 0.04],
)

# -- Three blocks (A, B, C) inside their respective bins -------------------
# 0.05m cubes, mass 0.1 kg, rubber material for friction-stacking.
block_specs = [
    ("/World/BlockA", [0.30, 0.40, 0.835], [[0.9, 0.2, 0.2]]),  # red
    ("/World/BlockB", [0.45, 0.20, 0.835], [[0.2, 0.7, 0.3]]),  # green
    ("/World/BlockC", [0.45, -0.20, 0.835], [[0.2, 0.3, 0.9]]),  # blue
]
block_paths = [spec[0] for spec in block_specs]

for path, pos, color in block_specs:
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=pos, size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)
    set_attribute(prim_path=path, attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=color)
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=block_paths,
    attr="physics:mass",
    value=0.1,
)
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=block_paths,
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)

# Top-down grasp pose for each block (Franka parallel-jaw approach from above).
for path in block_paths:
    define_grasp_pose(
        robot_path="/World/Franka",
        object_path=path,
        approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
        gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0],
    )

# -- STEPS 1+2: pick BlockA from Bin1, place on Station --------------------
# Both phases (pick + place) handled inside a single setup_pick_place_controller call.
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    robot_family="franka",
    source_paths=["/World/BlockA"],
    destination_path="/World/Station",
    drop_targets={"/World/BlockA": [-0.10, 0.30, 0.805]},
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Bin1", "/World/Bin2", "/World/Bin3", "/World/Station", "/World/OutfeedBin"],
)

# Settle 1s sim-time before sub-goal verification.
sim_control(action="step", step_count=60)

# Sub-goal #0 after step 2: BlockA z within [0.795, 0.815]. If fails, retry.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/BlockA")
check_collisions(prim_path="/World/BlockA")

execute_with_retry(
    code="setup_pick_place_controller(robot_path='/World/Franka', target_source='curobo', robot_family='franka', source_paths=['/World/BlockA'], destination_path='/World/Station', drop_targets={'/World/BlockA': [-0.10, 0.30, 0.805]}, planning_obstacles=['/World/Table', '/World/Bin1', '/World/Bin2', '/World/Bin3', '/World/Station', '/World/OutfeedBin'])",
    description="Step 1+2 retry: re-place BlockA on Station if sub-goal z-tolerance failed",
    max_retries=2,
)

# -- STEPS 3+4: pick BlockB from Bin2, place on top of BlockA --------------
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    robot_family="franka",
    source_paths=["/World/BlockB"],
    destination_path="/World/Station",
    drop_targets={"/World/BlockB": [-0.10, 0.30, 0.855]},
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Bin1", "/World/Bin2", "/World/Bin3", "/World/Station", "/World/OutfeedBin"],
)

sim_control(action="step", step_count=60)

# Step 5 — explicit sub-goal verification: 2-block stack height check.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/BlockB")
check_collisions(prim_path="/World/BlockB")

execute_with_retry(
    code="setup_pick_place_controller(robot_path='/World/Franka', target_source='curobo', robot_family='franka', source_paths=['/World/BlockB'], destination_path='/World/Station', drop_targets={'/World/BlockB': [-0.10, 0.30, 0.855]}, planning_obstacles=['/World/Table', '/World/Bin1', '/World/Bin2', '/World/Bin3', '/World/Station', '/World/OutfeedBin'])",
    description="Step 3+4 retry: re-place BlockB on BlockA if 2-block stack-height sub-goal failed",
    max_retries=2,
)

# -- STEPS 6+7: pick BlockC from Bin3, place on top of BlockB --------------
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    robot_family="franka",
    source_paths=["/World/BlockC"],
    destination_path="/World/Station",
    drop_targets={"/World/BlockC": [-0.10, 0.30, 0.905]},
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Bin1", "/World/Bin2", "/World/Bin3", "/World/Station", "/World/OutfeedBin"],
)

sim_control(action="step", step_count=60)

# Step 8 — 3-block stack stability check: z + collision-state on B and C.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/BlockC")
check_collisions(prim_path="/World/BlockC")
check_collisions(prim_path="/World/BlockB")

execute_with_retry(
    code="setup_pick_place_controller(robot_path='/World/Franka', target_source='curobo', robot_family='franka', source_paths=['/World/BlockC'], destination_path='/World/Station', drop_targets={'/World/BlockC': [-0.10, 0.30, 0.905]}, planning_obstacles=['/World/Table', '/World/Bin1', '/World/Bin2', '/World/Bin3', '/World/Station', '/World/OutfeedBin'])",
    description="Step 6+7 retry: re-place BlockC on BlockB if 3-block stack stability sub-goal failed",
    max_retries=2,
)

# -- STEPS 9+10: pick the assembled stack (grasp BlockA), place in OutfeedBin
# Grasping the bottom block lifts the whole tower because rubber friction +
# low approach velocity keeps blocks adhered as a unit.
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    robot_family="franka",
    source_paths=["/World/BlockA"],
    destination_path="/World/OutfeedBin",
    drop_targets={"/World/BlockA": [-0.30, -0.30, 0.805]},
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/Bin1", "/World/Bin2", "/World/Bin3", "/World/Station", "/World/OutfeedBin"],
)

sim_control(action="step", step_count=60)

# Final verification: all three blocks inside OutfeedBin footprint.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/BlockA")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/BlockB")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/BlockC")
check_collisions(prim_path="/World/BlockA")

execute_with_retry(
    code="setup_pick_place_controller(robot_path='/World/Franka', target_source='curobo', robot_family='franka', source_paths=['/World/BlockA'], destination_path='/World/OutfeedBin', drop_targets={'/World/BlockA': [-0.30, -0.30, 0.805]}, planning_obstacles=['/World/Table', '/World/Bin1', '/World/Bin2', '/World/Bin3', '/World/Station', '/World/OutfeedBin'])",
    description="Step 9+10 retry: re-transport assembled stack to OutfeedBin if final-placement sub-goal failed",
    max_retries=2,
)
failure_modes: Retry on physics-rooted failure simply repeats failure — if BlockB topples off BlockA because the stack-orientation drifted by >1 degree during release, replaying the same place-B-on-A code patch will produce the same toppled-stack outcome. The execute_with_retry autonomous fix loop must detect the underlying cause (e.g. read the post-failure transform of BlockA, compute the orientation drift, and emit a corrective re-place of BlockA before retrying step 4). Mitigation: provide context_hints=['stack-topple detection: read get_world_transform of bottom block; if rotation>1deg, re-place bottom block before retry'] to the execute_with_retry call.

Sub-goal threshold too tight — ±0.010m z-tolerance assumes cuRobo drop precision of 5-8mm + 1-2mm settle wobble. If the rubber-on-rubber friction coefficient causes a 3-5mm settle bounce (occasionally observed at low solver iter counts), valid placements will trigger false-positive retries. Mitigation: monitor settle_state.expected_retry_counts_nominal during smoke-test runs; if retries fire on >5% of nominal-success runs, loosen subgoal_z_tolerance to 0.015m.

Retry counter not reset between steps — the code calls execute_with_retry separately per step, so each call has its own max_retries=2 budget. However, if a future variant wraps the whole 10-step plan in a single execute_with_retry envelope (treating the whole plan as one patch), the retry counter would aggregate and a step-4 failure could exhaust the budget before step 7 even runs. The current per-step pattern is the correct shape; documented here to prevent regression during refactor.

Partial-progress recovery not handled — each retry replays only the failed step, not the upstream picks. If the failure was caused by upstream state corruption (e.g. BlockA shifted during the step-3 reach-over because the Franka swept too close to the station while approaching Bin2), retrying step 4 alone will hit the same failure. The LLM fix loop must detect this from get_world_transform output. Mitigation: in the execute_with_retry context_hints, instruct the fix loop to read transforms of ALL prior-placed blocks and emit corrective re-places when transforms have drifted >0.020m from the expected stack-coordinate.

Long-horizon timing budget — duration_s=700 covers the REPLAY-EVERY-STEP shape: each of the 4 phases runs the pick-place setup AND fires execute_with_retry unconditionally, so the effective phase count is ~8 (4 direct + 4 replay), each ~75s cuRobo plan+execute, plus 4 sub-goal settle pauses (4×1s) + retry slack (~80s). Total nominal envelope ~680s, with 700s as the budget. Mitigation: if a single phase exceeds 80s due to cuRobo solver stalls, extend duration_s to 800 for first-time runs; reduce after baseline timing established.

Unconditional-replay shape — by design each execute_with_retry call fires AFTER its corresponding setup_pick_place_controller regardless of the sub-goal probe outcome (get_world_transform + check_collisions are observational, not gating). This is 'replay every step for resilience', NOT 'retry on sub-goal failure': every step runs ~2x. The duration_s=700 budget accounts for this. Future variants that want true conditional retry must wrap each execute_with_retry call in a guard reading the prior get_world_transform z and firing the retry only when |z - expected_stack_z| > subgoal_z_tolerance.

Stack-pickup acceleration cap (step 9) — to harden against future motion-profile changes, the canonical SHOULD prepend set_motion_policy(robot_path='/World/Franka', max_accel=1.0) before step 9. Current rubber-on-rubber friction (~1.2) × g >> 2 m/s² cuRobo default acceleration, so the assembled stack holds at the default profile. A future tighter cycle-time profile that bumps max accel above ~1.0 m/s² will cause BlockC to slide off — the explicit set_motion_policy is the defensive guard.

Block-on-block stacking precision — z drop targets are computed by stacking 0.05m blocks: BlockA at 0.805 (centre), BlockB at 0.855 (centre = A_top + B/2 = 0.830 + 0.025), BlockC at 0.905. cuRobo drop heights add a small approach offset (~0.005-0.010m) which is absorbed by the rubber friction and settle gravity, but a calibration drift in the cuRobo gripper-tip frame can produce a 0.005-0.010m systematic z-error that compounds across the stack. Mitigation: after step 4, compare BlockB.z to BlockA.z+0.050; if delta > 0.060m, the gripper-tip frame is mis-calibrated — emit a re-calibration step before continuing.

Step 9 'pick the assembled stack' relies on rubber friction holding the 3-block tower together during lift — Franka grasps BlockA (bottom) and the other two ride along. If acceleration during lift exceeds friction × g, BlockC may slide off the top during transport. Verified shape: low-velocity lift with cuRobo default trapezoidal profile, max acceleration ~2 m/s², friction coefficient rubber-on-rubber ~1.2 → friction force >> gravity → stack holds. If a future variant uses higher-velocity profile, BlockC will slide; add an explicit set_motion_policy(robot_path, max_accel=1.0) before step 9.

Bin-arc layout reach margin — Bin2/Bin3 at horizontal distance sqrt(0.45^2+0.20^2)=0.49m and Outfeed at sqrt(0.30^2+0.30^2)=0.42m, all well within Franka 0.85m reach. Station at sqrt(0.10^2+0.30^2)=0.32m is the closest. No tight-margin risks; documented for completeness.

execute_with_retry semantics — max_retries=2 means the system attempts the patch up to 3 times total (1 initial + 2 retries). If a step succeeds on retry 1 or 2, the canonical continues to the next step. If all retries exhaust, the canonical reports the per-step failure but does NOT auto-abort — downstream steps still execute (best-effort continuation). This is intentional: it lets the function-gate observe partial-progress outcomes. If a stricter abort-on-step-failure semantics is wanted, wrap each execute_with_retry call in a guard that calls cancel_workflow when the retry budget exhausts.
extension_notes: First long-horizon (10-step) canonical wired with execute_with_retry per-step. The current shape is REPLAY-EVERY-STEP (unconditional retry envelope after every pick-place phase, providing 2x resilience against intermittent cuRobo stalls); duration_s=700 covers the resulting ~8 effective pick-place phases. A future conditional-retry variant would gate each execute_with_retry on the prior get_world_transform z reading against subgoal_z_tolerance — that variant should drop duration_s back toward 450s and be tagged isaac:planning.retry_conditional. Future hardening axes: (a) prepend set_motion_policy(max_accel=1.0) before step 9 to defend the assembled-stack lift against future motion-profile changes, (b) wrap the execute_with_retry calls in cancel_workflow guards if abort-on-budget-exhaust semantics is wanted, (c) extend the retry context_hints to include get_world_transform inspections of upstream blocks for partial-progress recovery (documented in failure_modes #4).
code_template:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.6, 0.4, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})
robot_wizard(robot_name={{primary_robot.class}}, dest_path={{primary_robot.path}}, position={{primary_robot.position}}, orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}})
create_bin(prim_path={{source_bins[0].path}}, position={{source_bins[0].position}}, size={{source_bins[0].size}})
create_bin(prim_path={{source_bins[1].path}}, position={{source_bins[1].position}}, size={{source_bins[1].size}})
create_bin(prim_path={{source_bins[2].path}}, position={{source_bins[2].position}}, size={{source_bins[2].size}})
create_prim(prim_path={{stack_station.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{stack_station.position}}, scale={{stack_station.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{stack_station.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_bin(prim_path={{outfeed_destination.path}}, position={{outfeed_destination.position}}, size={{outfeed_destination.size}})
create_prim(prim_path={{blocks[0].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{blocks[0].position}}, size={{blocks[0].size}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{blocks[0].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{blocks[0].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{blocks[0].path}}, schema_name="PhysicsMassAPI")
